statement.markdown: Given a set of non-overlapping intervals, you are asked to insert a new interval and merge it with existing intervals that overlap with it. The new interval is given by the start and end indices. You can assume that the input intervals are sorted in ascending order and no duplicate intervals are present.
statement.inputFormat: The input will contain a list of start and end indices representing the intervals.
statement.outputFormat: The output should be the result after merging the new interval with the existing intervals.
statement.notes: The new interval may not necessarily be added to the end of the existing list. It might be merged with some interval(s) at any position within the list.

examples.explanation: The new interval [3,5] overlaps with the existing intervals [1,3] and [2,6]. After merging, the result becomes [1,6].

hints.text: Sort the existing intervals to simplify the merging process.

editorial.title: Merging Non-Overlapping Intervals after Adding a New One
editorial.intuition: To solve this problem efficiently, we need to sort the existing intervals and the new interval. Then, we can merge any overlapping pairs of intervals.
editorial.bruteForce: This problem can be solved by iterating over the existing intervals and adding the new interval to the list. However, this will result in a time complexity of O(n^2).
editorial.betterSolution: We can sort the existing intervals and the new interval, and then merge any overlapping pairs of intervals.
editorial.optimalSolution: To implement the optimal solution, we can use a simple iterative approach. We iterate over the existing intervals and check for any overlaps. If we find an overlap, we merge the two intervals.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of the optimal solution can be proven by showing that it correctly handles all possible cases, including the edge cases and the time complexity.
editorial.dryRun: Let's consider an example where we have the following intervals: [1,3],[2,6],[8,10],[15,18]. We can sort them and then merge any overlapping pairs. After merging, we get: [1,6],[8,10],[15,18].
commonMistakes: Not sorting the intervals before merging
